- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/iRjUXB5TACf1DRS9GS0K_image.jpg](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/iRjUXB5TACf1DRS9GS0K_image.jpg)

---

##    17\. The "Life Happens" Protocol 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/FpaIFZwHRumUzQkRrKoD_ayla.jpeg)

I'm guessing these next 3-4 months will be crucial for you?

You've made promises to yourself. You're going to be more disciplined. You're going to take things more seriously. But I ask you…

  
How do you know that this isn't just your seasonal optimism?  
  
Here's one way to find out... Answer this question:  
  
"What will you do when you get derailed?"  
  
_Notice I didn't ask "if". I asked WHEN._  
  
Artists who are truly READY have something the others don't:  
  
_They have a 'Life Happens' Protocol._  
  
**Bank on it… Something unexpected will happen at some point in the future that will derail your art-making. Things will get in the way of your discipline. It is law.**  
  
But artists who have prepared for when 'Life Happens'… They don't crumble. They don't scramble. They don't succumb. They are ready.  
  
They have a protocol.  


![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/PfT5HAxvSpCrzlrhXDyg_travis+life+happens.jpeg)

**The 'Life Happens' Protocol**  


What is a protocol?

It's "a system of rules that explain the correct conduct and procedures to follow in certain situations".

When "life happens"-- For example... You get sick. Or a family crisis comes up. Or, you get hit by a wave of numbing depression...

You need to have your protocol ready to execute.

Here is an example of what it might say…

===

_Dear  X,_

_Life happens._

_But I want to remind you that you are still a superstar artist in the making._

_Everything you do in this intense period will be part of your story. It will be where you will draw anecdotes for future interviews. It will be fodder for acceptance speeches.    
  
So, no need to panic. No need to despair.  
  
Just follow the protocol:   
  
1\. This is what you should eat to stay healthy in this challenging time: (examples of: your comfort foods, your favorite fruits)  
  
2\. This is how you should take care of yourbody to keep your physical and mental strength up. Simple rituals: (examples: stretches in the morning, walk at night, meditation)   
  
3\. This is how you should reduce your art-making work-load for this crazy time so you still feel like you're making progress: (example: daily practice reduced to 15 minutes, research and organizing that you never had time to do before)  
  
4\. Stay in the community: (examples: reach out to collaborators, read artist biographies)  
  
Use this time wisely.  
  
Use it fully._  
  
===

**Print it out.  
  
Date it.  
  
Sign it.  
  
Place it in a sealed envelope.**  
  
**Address it to: TO MY FUTURE SELF: The 'Life Happens' Protocol**  
  
  
And then place it in the bottom of a drawer for the day you'll need it.

Today, you will have taken a step you have never taken before. You will have prepared yourself for the unknown. You will have created an insurance policy for your art.

NOW I believe you.

_NOW you can believe yourself._

_Here is an example of a "Life Happens" protocol that a student sent me. It had me in tears._

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/g3v7oxQtmCWfRzDH5bWw_LIFExHAPPENS.jpg)

Dear Parker,

Life happens.

But I want to remind you that you are already an influential, showstopping artist, and you cannot stop now.

Everything you do in this break out period is part of your story. Every moment is a prized gem of future talk show interviews. It will be fodder for acceptance speeches.

No need to panic or withdraw.

Follow the plan:

**I. This is what you should eat to stay healthy in this challenging time: Water with electrolytes, grapefruit, tofu, collards, slaw, hopping john salads**

**II. Simple rituals, meditation and exercise will make you feel better than sleeping-in or drugs. Stretch in the morning. Breath in to allow. Breathe out to nurture.**

**III. Reduce your load.Use crochet, reading or skating to return to flow.**

**IV. Stay in the community by being honest about life's struggles.**

I love you endlessly. It's just for fun.

Parker

===

**Be accountable. Send me a picture of your sealed 'Life Happens Protocol' envelope!**

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/wWAcW6tSSoiQTETWWskV_lifehappenscollage.png)

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

Amanda Van Helsdingen

Awaiting Review 

a year ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/49639533/comments/26294329)

I forgot about my 'Life Happens Protocol' until just now! I just opened it! It is exactly what I need! I have been in a 'Life Happens' void for the past three weeks and I just decided to log back onto Mystery School tonight (after a fellow GM student reached out to me on IG) in the hope of finding my momentum. I saw the 14. 'Life Happens" Protocol and remembered I created the envelope of 'artist insurance'. I went to my wardrobe and found it! I wish I had remembered I had this and opened it three weeks ago!!! I wrote it almost exactly one year ago (I noticed the date of 21st of May 2024) and I was surprised and pleased with what I had written - especially the "this is what you should eat to stay healthy in this challenging time." So glad I found this right now. My protocol includes an important message: to 'just draw a leaf' which is apt - as I just drew a leaf today after a three week stall...ok - I'm going to be ok! (I'll follow the protocol and then reseal the envelope and put it away to be accessed again when needed!)

![Add Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)




![quintensol](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/0ba69aa2f21b23b33ff89a7d2916f6f5?d=mm&s=70)

quintensol

Awaiting Review 

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/49639533/comments/24445844)

WHEN LIFE HAPPENS. zoals john lennon het zei: "life is what happens when you're busy making other plans". juist wanneer je tegenslag ervaart, juist wanneer het tegenzit, is het van belang dat je bewust blijft en bij zinnen. dat zijn de momenten waar je juist moet gronden, dat zijn de momenten waar anderen opgeven. precies deze momenten zijn cruciaal voor je. hoe ga je met die tegenslag om? hoe kan je er anders naar kijken? wat moet je anders doen? je hebt al eerder moeilijke tijden doorstaan. je zette door met informatica... zelfs je pa stopte na een jaar. ookal ben je later gestopt wegens goede redenen. je gaf niet op na je HKU afwijzing en je deed voor de tweede keer je toelating, waarna je bent aangenomen. jij hebt de hoop, het karakter, de visie om te komen waar je wilt, maar je moet in jezelf blijven geloven, en goed voor jezelf blijven zorgen. hieronder staan tips om jezelf vast te houden tijdens deze moeilijke tijd. Lichamelijk I. Slaap regelmatig. 23.30 tot 8.00. Ritme is belangrijker dan hoeveelheid. II. Eet voldoende. Vers fruit en vitamines. Zorg dat je vol zit. III. Beweging. Elke ochtend een korte wandeling. Elke woensdag en zaterdag een eindje hardlopen. Bewustzijn IV. Bewustzijn. Wees bewust van je gedachten. Wees bewust van wat je gedachten kunnen veroorzaken. Wees verantwoordelijk voor waar je aandacht en energie aan besteedt. Energy flows where attention goes. V. Mentaal dieet. Niet gedachtenloos informatie binnenschrokken. Niet doomscrollen. Niet alles verdient je aandacht en energie. Consumeer bewust. VI. Aandacht. Je hebt ADHD en ASD; moeite met concentreren, snel afgeleid, impulsief, prikkelbaar, lage aandachtsspanne. Vergeef jezelf. Voor jou is dit gewoon wat lastiger. Kijk bij de andere lijst voor activiteiten waarbij je je aandacht terugpakt. Gevoel VII. Intuïtie. Luister naar wat je lichaam jou probeert te vertellen. Daar moet je stil, aandachtig en rustig voor zijn. Als je onderbuik jou iets vertelt, dan weet je hart vaak meer dan je brein. VIII. Respecteer je gevoelens. Geen schuldgevoel voor hoe je je voelt. Geen schuldgevoel voor wanneer je anderen IX. Boundaries. Geef je grenzen aan. Daar moet je goed voor luisteren naar jezelf, tune into yourself. Zeg; gaat je niks aan; daar heb ik het liever niet over; en als ze volhardend zijn, dan sla je ze weg... en wees eerlijk tegen ze over wat je wilt. Spreek jezelf uit! X. Guilttrip. Laat je je niet zo meevoeren door schuld. Je bent mensen niks verschuldigd, jij staat in de macht. Liefde XI. De liefde die je kan nemen, staat gelijk aan de liefde die je kan geven. Geef aandacht aan je vrienden en familie. Geef aandacht aan je hobbies, aan wat de ander zegt. Luister naar ze, wacht niet enkel maar totdat je je eigen woord kan doen. XII. Love your neighbour, don't worship them, don't hate them. Liefde komt uit spontaniteit, niet uit schuldgevoel. en als het echt slecht gaat.... maaltijdsalades & plenny shakes als het echt slecht gaat. ochtendwandeling na ontbijt. hardlopen woensdag en zaterdag. thee, niet te veel caffeïne. praten met mam/yen/aleja daily creative practice!!! flowstate: lezen, gitaarspelen, afspreken met een vriend stoom afblazen: bel een vriend, venting, laat die spanning op je schouders los. afleiding (niet te veel) durf jezelf rust te gunnen. het is een voorwaarde om goed te kunnen functioneren, niet een beloning. uitslapen helpt niet; routine in je slaapritme wel. wat consumeer je? qua social media, series, boeken? het vormt je. langzaam maar zeker; of het nou lang of kort duurt. neem alle tijd die je nodig hebt. bewaak je persoonlijke grenzen, naar jezelf en naar anderen.

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/49639533/comments/23588813)

I’ve seriously crashed and burned twice over the past 6 years on this creative journey and this simple little note, is the answer to catch it and potentially stop it from happening again. This note feels like freedom and permission to be a creative beast while recognizing that I am a mom with little humans need me to be okay for them. Thank you.

* * *

  * [ ![](https://static-media.hotmart.com/-IX-QSvUP8p471GkemyaNrC0A6o=/200x200/https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/GNYCoxMnSeqKLbIvKayg_IMG_7066.jpeg) ](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/GNYCoxMnSeqKLbIvKayg_IMG_7066.jpeg)



![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/49639533/comments/23465342)

1\. This is what you should eat to stay healthy in this challenging time: (examples of: your comfort foods, your favorite fruits) Eggs, milk, juice Salad, grape tomato, avocado, broccoli (pick 3) Sushi, poke bowl, salmon, tuna, sardine, mussel Honeycrisp apple, tangerine, grape, mango, banana, cantaloupe potato bread, cream of wheat, breakfast essentials, trail mix, cereal one-a-day vitamin, one gallon of water Truffle mushroom pizza when they have it, coffee 1mdd 2\. This is how you should take care of your body to keep your physical and mental strength up. Simple rituals: (examples: stretches in the morning, walk at night, meditation) Make Sunday a day of worship, work the fuck out Walk to work every day Sleep 7-11 hours a night Drink once a week (if at all) Smoke twice a week (if at all) Do not eat after 10pm Try to stretch 3\. This is how you should reduce your art-making work-load for this crazy time so you still feel like you're making progress: (example: daily practice reduced to 15 minutes, research and organizing that you never had time to do before) Complete daily Codecademy + Duolingo Write one 8 or 16-bar verse Design one article for ugli or boytoy Write one page of ugli or uglimag Post once on social media Talk to someone new, talk to someone old (if at all) Cry, read 4\. Stay in the community: (examples: reach out to collaborators, read artist biographies) Listen to a new album, watch a new movie or episode Build a spreadsheet of projects and people that fit necessary roles Spend 30 minutes a day, five days a week, on someone else’s project Purchase Rochester Artists Collaborative membership Go to local concerts nearby, bring someone Cold DM 3 creatives, ask if there’s anything to assist or collaborate on Comment on nineteen posts, less than 1 hour social media use per day
